name: Indonesian
register: Reverent contemporary Indonesian in the wording family of the Lembaga Alkitab Indonesia Terjemahan Baru (TB, 1974) — the most-trusted and most-loved Indonesian Bible across Protestant and Catholic communities — updated to modern spelling in the manner of TB2 (2023). This is the dignified-yet-warm register of Scripture read aloud in worship and prayer.
quote_convention: Double curly quotation marks “…” for speech, single ‘…’ for a quote nested inside a quote; direct speech introduced with a comma or colon (… kata-Nya, “…”).
poetry_convention: Poetry is set as line-broken verse with parallel cola (as in the TB Psalms and Proverbs); each poetic line starts a new line and the second colon of a couplet is indented. No rhyme or meter is imposed — Hebrew parallelism is preserved.

glossary.note: Anchored to Terjemahan Baru (Mt 6:9) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Bapa Surgawi kita". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Primary filial divine name. Uses inclusive 'kita' to draw the reader in as a fellow child. NOTE FOR REVIEWER: the community's most-loved rendering in direct address/prayer is the Lord's-Prayer form 'Bapa kami' (exclusive 'kami', as in 'Bapa kami yang di sorga'); reviewer to set the kita/kami policy for narration vs. address. Never revert to Allah/TUHAN.

Anchored to Terjemahan Baru (Jn 14:9ff) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Bapa". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to Terjemahan Baru (Mt 6:14) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Bapa Surgawi". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Retains the Aramaic 'Abba' + vocative 'ya' (O) + 'Bapa'; matches the TB rendering of Rom 8:15 / Gal 4:6 / Mark 14:36 exactly.
NON-FILIAL CONTRAST TERM. The standard LAI/TB word for God-as-God. Use ONLY where English keeps 'God' as deity; never as a substitute for 'Bapa kita'.
NON-FILIAL CONTRAST TERM. All-caps 'TUHAN' is the LAI convention for YHWH, distinct from title 'Tuhan' (Lord) used for Jesus. Use ONLY where English retains the covenant name.
Standard TB term; confirmed in preview (John 3).
Relational (the Spirit belonging to the Father), not the by-identity title 'Roh Kudus'. Carries the 'Bapa' stem; per preview (Gen 1:2).
Fixed TB Christological/self-designation title; confirmed in preview (John 3).
Honorific 'Sang' + 'Anak' marks the definite, reverent 'the Son' (Jesus) and distinguishes it from ordinary 'anak' (child); bare 'Anak' as in TB is also acceptable.

Traditional TB confessional title. REVIEWER TO CONFIRM vs. a filial variant 'Anak Bapa' (Son of the Father), paralleling the Telugu తండ్రి కుమారుడు; kept as 'Anak Allah' where the fixed creedal title is intended.
TB John 3:16 wording; the possessive '-Nya' refers to the Father. Where no possessor is present, 'Anak Tunggal' (the only Son). Confirmed in preview (John 3:16).
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially; replaces TB 'Kerajaan Allah'. Carries the 'Bapa' stem; confirmed in preview (John 3:3,5).
Standard TB spelling; confirmed in preview.
Standard TB term; confirmed in preview (John 3:28).
Standard TB term (John 1:41; 4:25).
Standard TB term for both the old and new covenant (perjanjian lama / baru).
Renders Hebrew hesed. 'kasih setia' is exactly the TB rendering of loyal covenant love — confirmed as the natural match.
Renders racham / splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy); standard TB rendering, warmer than 'kasihan' alone.
TB renders tsedeq/dikaiosyne as 'kebenaran'. NOTE: the same word also means 'truth' — reviewer to confirm context does not need 'keadilan' (justice) where the forensic/just sense dominates.

glossary.gloss: life that is eternal / everlasting life
glossary.note: TB John 3:16 wording ('beroleh hidup yang kekal'); confirmed in preview.
TB John 3:3,7 wording. The Greek anōthen also carries 'born from above' (dilahirkan dari atas); TB and the preview keep 'dilahirkan kembali'.
